 {"id":"bd-29lr","title":"Phase 1: Foundation - fastapi-output Crate and Core Infrastructure","description":"# Phase 1: Foundation

## Purpose

Establish the core infrastructure for rich output integration by creating the new fastapi-output 
crate with agent detection, mode switching, and the RichOutput facade API.

## Why This Phase First

Everything else depends on this foundation:
- Agent detection determines which mode to use
- OutputMode enum is used by all components
- RichOutput facade is the unified API all other phases call
- Theme system defines colors used everywhere

Without this phase, no other rich output work can proceed.

## Deliverables

1. **New crate: fastapi-output** (crates/fastapi-output/)
   - Cargo.toml with rich_rust as optional dependency
   - Module structure for all subcomponents
   - Re-exports for convenient API access

2. **Agent Detection System** (detection.rs)
   - is_agent_environment() function
   - Environment variable checks (CLAUDE_CODE, CODEX_CLI, etc.)
   - TTY detection via crossterm
   - NO_COLOR standard support
   - FORCE_COLOR override

3. **OutputMode System** (mode.rs)
   - OutputMode enum (Rich, Plain, Minimal)
   - Mode selection logic
   - Runtime mode switching support

4. **RichOutput Facade** (facade.rs)
   - Central RichOutput struct
   - Mode-aware method dispatching
   - Global instance management (lazy static)
   - Builder pattern for configuration

5. **Theme System** (themes.rs)
   - FastApiTheme struct with all color definitions
   - ThemePreset enum for built-in themes
   - Color constants for HTTP methods, status codes
   - Theme loading and application

## Technical Decisions

### Why a Separate Crate?

1. **Modularity**: Can be disabled entirely via feature flag
2. **Compile Time**: Only compiled when needed
3. **Dependency Isolation**: rich_rust dependency contained
4. **Testing**: Can test output logic independently

### Why Optional rich_rust Dependency?

When the "rich" feature is disabled:
- No rich_rust code compiled
- Plain mode is the only mode
- Minimal binary size impact
- Zero runtime overhead

### Global Instance vs Passed Reference?

Using a global lazy_static instance because:
- Output happens everywhere in the codebase
- Passing RichOutput through every function is impractical
- One-time initialization is fine for this use case
- Thread-safe via LazyLock

## Files to Create

```
crates/fastapi-output/
├── Cargo.toml
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs           # Public API, prelude
│   ├── detection.rs     # Agent environment detection
│   ├── mode.rs          # OutputMode enum and logic
│   ├── facade.rs        # RichOutput struct
│   └── themes.rs        # Theme definitions
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `cargo build -p fastapi-output` succeeds
2. `cargo build -p fastapi-output --no-default-features` succeeds (plain mode only)
3. Agent detection correctly identifies Claude Code, Codex, etc.
4. Mode switching works at runtime
5. Theme colors render correctly in rich mode
6.  {"id":"bd-2buz","title":"Implement OpenAPI endpoint table display","description":"## Task: OpenAPI Endpoint Table Display

### Parent Feature
Phase 5: OpenAPI Display & Visual Polish (bd-urty)

### Overview
Create a visual table display of all OpenAPI-documented endpoints, showing methods, paths, summaries, and tags in a scannable format.

### Background & Reasoning
When developing APIs, having a quick overview of all endpoints helps:
- Verify all routes are documented
- Check for naming consistency
- Understand API scope at a glance
- Debug missing endpoints

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structures
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/openapi_display.rs

pub struct OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
    pub endpoints: Vec<EndpointInfo>,
    pub group_by_tag: bool,
    pub show_responses: bool,
}

pub struct EndpointInfo {
    pub method: HttpMethod,
    pub path: String,
    pub operation_id: Option<String>,
    pub summary: Option<String>,
    pub tags: Vec<String>,
    pub deprecated: bool,
    pub auth_required: bool,
    pub response_codes: Vec<u16>,
}
```

#### Visualization

**Rich Mode**:
```

│ │ POST   │ /items              │ Create an item        🔒     ││
│ │ GET    │ /items/{id}         │ Get item by ID               ││
│ └────────┴─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┘│

```

**Plain Mode**:

### Implementation
```rust
impl OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        let mut content = Vec::new();
        
        content.push(String::new());
        content.push(format!("  {} endpoints documented", self.endpoints.len()));
        content.push(String::new());
        
        // Group by tag if enabled
        let groups = if self.group_by_tag {
            self.group_endpoints_by_tag()
        } else {
            vec![("All".to_string(), self.endpoints.clone())]
        };
        
        for (tag, endpoints) in groups {
            if self.group_by_tag {
                content.push(theme.style_text(&format!("  {}", tag), "bold"));
            }
            
            let mut table = Table::new()
                .with_column(Column::new("").width(8))
                .with_column(Column::new("").width(25))
                .with_column(Column::new("").max_width(40));
            
            for ep in &endpoints {
                let method_styled = theme.style_method(&ep.method);
                
                let mut summary = ep.summary.clone().unwrap_or_default();
                
                // Add indicators
                let mut indicators = Vec::new();
                if ep.auth_required {
                    indicators.push("🔒");
                }
                if ep.deprecated {
                    indicators.push("⚠");
                }
                
                if !indicators.is_empty() {
                    summary = format!("{} {}", summary, indicators.join(" "));
                }
                
                table.add_row_cells([
                    method_styled,
                    ep.path.clone(),
                    summary,
                ]);
            }
            
            content.push(format!("{}", table.render_to_string()));
            content.push(String::new());
        }
        
        // Legend
        content.push(theme.style_text("  🔒 = Authentication required", "dim"));
        content.push(theme.style_text("  ⚠ = Deprecated", "dim"));
        content.push(String::new());
        
        let panel = Panel::from_text(&content.join("\n"))
            .title("API Endpoints")
            .rounded();
        
        output.renderable(&panel);
    }
}
```

### Integration with OpenAPI Crate
```rust
// In fastapi-openapi/src/lib.rs

impl OpenApiSpec {
    pub fn display_endpoints(&self) {
        let endpoints: Vec<EndpointInfo> = self.paths.iter()
            .flat_map(|(path, ops)| {
                ops.iter().map(|(method, op)| EndpointInfo {
                    method: method.clone(),
                    path: path.clone(),
                    operation_id: op.operation_id.clone(),
                    summary: op.summary.clone(),
                    tags: op.tags.clone(),
                    deprecated: op.deprecated,
                    auth_required: !op.security.is_empty(),
                    response_codes: op.responses.keys().cloned().collect(),
                })
            })
            .collect();
        
        OpenApiEndpointDisplay {
            endpoints,
            group_by_tag: true,
            show_responses: false,
        }.display();
    }
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EndpointInfo data structure
- [ ] Table display with method coloring
- [ ] Tag-based grouping (optional)

- [ ] Deprecation indicator (⚠)
- [ ] Endpoint count summary
- [ ] Legend for indicators
- [ ] Plain text fallback
- [ ] Integration with OpenAPI spec
- [ ] Unit tests

 {"id":"bd-urty","title":"Phase 5: OpenAPI Display & Visual Polish","description":"## Feature: OpenAPI Display & Visual Polish

### Parent Epic
Rich Output Integration: Premium Console UI with Agent Compatibility (bd-nww8)

### Overview
This phase focuses on OpenAPI schema visualization and overall visual polish across the framework, ensuring consistent premium aesthetics throughout.

### Background & Reasoning
OpenAPI documentation is a key feature of FastAPI-style frameworks. Visual display of the schema during development helps:
- Verify endpoint documentation is correct
- Debug schema generation issues
- Understand API structure at a glance

Additionally, this phase addresses visual consistency and polish across all components.

### Components in This Phase

#### 1. OpenAPI Schema Display
Visual representation of generated OpenAPI schema showing:
- Endpoint summaries
- Request/response schemas
- Authentication requirements
- Example values

#### 2. Schema Type Visualization  
Display complex JSON Schema types:
- Object properties
- Array types
- Enum values
- Nested schemas

#### 3. Console Theme Refinement
Polish the theme system:
- Consistent color palette
- Box style consistency
- Spacing and alignment
- Dark/light theme variants

#### 4. Help & Usage Display
Beautiful help text and usage information:
- Command-line argument help
- Configuration options
- Quick reference guides

### Technical Considerations

#### OpenAPI Display Strategy
```rust
// Show endpoint summary table
┌──────────────────── API Endpoints ────────────────────┐
│ Method │ Path              │ Summary                  │
├────────┼───────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
│ GET    │ /users            │ List all users           │
│ POST   │ /users            │ Create a new user        │
│ GET    │ /users/{id}       │ Get user by ID           │
│ PUT    │ /users/{id}       │ Update user              │
│ DELETE │ /users/{id}       │ Delete user              │
```

 {"id":"bd-ycu4","title":"Implement debug logging infrastructure for rich output","description":"# Implement Debug Logging Infrastructure

## Task Overview

Add comprehensive debug logging throughout the rich output system to aid in debugging
mode detection, output rendering, and integration issues.

## Why Debug Logging?

When things go wrong (wrong mode detected, unexpected output, etc.), we need to be able to:
- See what mode was detected and why
- Track which environment variables were checked
- Understand the rendering path taken
- Verify output was captured correctly in tests

## File: crates/fastapi-output/src/debug.rs

```rust
//\! Debug logging infrastructure for rich output.
//\!
//\! This module provides verbose debug logging that can be enabled via
//\! FASTAPI_OUTPUT_DEBUG=1 to help diagnose mode detection and rendering issues.

use std::env;
use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, Ordering};

/// Global flag for debug logging.
static DEBUG_ENABLED: AtomicBool = AtomicBool::new(false);

/// Initialize debug logging from environment.
pub fn init() {
    let enabled = env::var("FASTAPI_OUTPUT_DEBUG")
        .map(|v| v == "1" || v.to_lowercase() == "true")
        .unwrap_or(false);
    DEBUG_ENABLED.store(enabled, Ordering::SeqCst);
    if enabled {
        eprintln\!("[FASTAPI_OUTPUT] Debug logging enabled");
    }
}

/// Check if debug logging is enabled.
pub fn is_debug_enabled() -> bool {
    DEBUG_ENABLED.load(Ordering::SeqCst)
}

/// Enable debug logging programmatically (for tests).
pub fn enable_debug() {
    DEBUG_ENABLED.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst);
}

/// Disable debug logging programmatically (for tests).
pub fn disable_debug() {
    DEBUG_ENABLED.store(false, Ordering::SeqCst);
}

/// Log a debug message if debug logging is enabled.
#[macro_export]
macro_rules\! debug_log {
    ($($arg:tt)*) => {
        if $crate::debug::is_debug_enabled() {
            eprintln\!("[FASTAPI_OUTPUT] {}", format\!($($arg)*));
        }
    };
}

/// Log detection-related debug info.
#[macro_export]
macro_rules\! debug_detection {
    ($($arg:tt)*) => {
        if $crate::debug::is_debug_enabled() {
            eprintln\!("[FASTAPI_OUTPUT:DETECT] {}", format\!($($arg)*));
        }
    };
}

/// Log mode-related debug info.
#[macro_export]
macro_rules\! debug_mode {
    ($($arg:tt)*) => {
        if $crate::debug::is_debug_enabled() {
            eprintln\!("[FASTAPI_OUTPUT:MODE] {}", format\!($($arg)*));
        }
    };
}

/// Log rendering-related debug info.
#[macro_export]
macro_rules\! debug_render {
    ($($arg:tt)*) => {
        if $crate::debug::is_debug_enabled() {
            eprintln\!("[FASTAPI_OUTPUT:RENDER] {}", format\!($($arg)*));
        }
    };
}

/// Log test-related debug info.
#[macro_export]
macro_rules\! debug_test {
    ($($arg:tt)*) => {
        if $crate::debug::is_debug_enabled() {
            eprintln\!("[FASTAPI_OUTPUT:TEST] {}", format\!($($arg)*));
        }
    };
}
```

## Usage Examples

### In detection.rs

```rust
use crate::{debug_detection, debug_log};

pub fn is_agent_environment() -> bool {
    debug_detection\!("Checking agent environment...");
    
    for var in AGENT_ENV_VARS {
        if env::var(var).is_ok() {
            debug_detection\!("Found agent env var: {}", var);
            return true;
        }
    }
    
    debug_detection\!("No agent env vars found");
    false
}
```

### In mode.rs

```rust
use crate::debug_mode;

impl OutputMode {
    pub fn auto() -> Self {
        debug_mode\!("OutputMode::auto() called");
        
        if let Ok(mode_str) = env::var("FASTAPI_OUTPUT_MODE") {
            debug_mode\!("Found FASTAPI_OUTPUT_MODE={}", mode_str);
            if let Ok(mode) = mode_str.parse() {
                debug_mode\!("Parsed mode: {:?}", mode);
                return mode;
            }
        }
        
        let detected = is_agent_environment();
        debug_mode\!("Agent detection result: {}", detected);
        
        if detected {
            debug_mode\!("Using Plain mode (agent detected)");
            OutputMode::Plain
        } else {
            debug_mode\!("Using Rich mode (human terminal)");
            OutputMode::Rich
        }
    }
}
```

### In facade.rs

```rust
use crate::debug_render;

impl RichOutput {
    pub fn success(&self, message: &str) {
        debug_render\!("success() mode={:?} msg={}", self.mode, message);
        self.status(StatusKind::Success, message);
    }
    
    fn write_line(&self, text: &str) {
        debug_render\!("write_line() len={}", text.len());
        // ...
    }
}
```

### In tests

```rust
use crate::{debug_test, debug};

#[test]
fn test_with_debug() {
    debug::enable_debug();
    debug_test\!("Starting test_with_debug");
    
    let mode = OutputMode::auto();
    debug_test\!("Got mode: {:?}", mode);
    
    // Test assertions...
    
    debug::disable_debug();
}
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Effect |
|----------|--------|
| `FASTAPI_OUTPUT_DEBUG=1` | Enable all debug logging |
| `FASTAPI_OUTPUT_DEBUG=true` | Enable all debug logging |
| Unset/other | Debug logging disabled |

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_debug_disabled_by_default() {
        disable_debug();
        assert\!(\!is_debug_enabled());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_enable_disable_debug() {
        disable_debug();
        assert\!(\!is_debug_enabled());
        
        enable_debug();
        assert\!(is_debug_enabled());
        
        disable_debug();
        assert\!(\!is_debug_enabled());
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_debug_log_macro_when_disabled() {
        disable_debug();
        // Should not panic or print
        debug_log\!("This should not appear");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_debug_log_macro_when_enabled() {
        enable_debug();
        // Should print to stderr
        debug_log\!("Test message: {}", 42);
        disable_debug();
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_all_debug_macros() {
        enable_debug();
        debug_detection\!("detection test");
        debug_mode\!("mode test");
        debug_render\!("render test");
        debug_test\!("test test");
        disable_debug();
    }
}
```

- testing.rs: debug_test\!
